sections.title: Talking to any Mysterious ghost without a ghostspeak amulet

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Wooo? Woooo woo woooooo wooooo woooowooo wooo woooooo woo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I heard about that, ha-ha-ha!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Woo! WOO WOOOOO WOOWOOWOO WOO WOOOOO!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: With a MACKEREL? Ouch!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Wooowoowoooooo.... Woowoowoo? Woooo, wooowoo wooowooowooo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it was fun. Let's do it again sometime.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, don't think you can talk to me like that!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Woo! WOO WOOOOO WOOWOOWOO WOO WOOOOO!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you threatening me?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Wooowoowoooooo.... Woowoowoo? Woooo, wooowoo wooowooowooo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just because you're already dead, doesn't mean I can't find a way to hurt you ghosty!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, thank you very much!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Woo! WOO WOOOOO WOOWOOWOO WOO WOOOOO!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know, but what are you going to do?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Wooowoowoooooo.... Woowoowoo? Woooo, wooowoo wooowooowooo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I guess that's always true in the long run. See you around, weird invisible dead person!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We get signal!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Woo! WOO WOOOOO WOOWOOWOO WOO WOOOOO!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Somebody set up us the bomb!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Wooowoowoooooo.... Woowoowoo? Woooo, wooowoo wooowooowooo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have no chance to survive make your time.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Woo?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All your base are belong to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I've never been there in my life and CERTAINLY didn't steal anything when I was!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Woo! WOO WOOOOO WOOWOOWOO WOO WOOOOO!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you calling me a liar?!?! I have never stolen a thing in my life, and I resent the implication that I am the kind of morally depraved individual that would steal someone else's hard earned

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: money from their very pockets!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Or cakes. Or fur. Repeatedly. For long periods at a time.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Wooowoowoooooo.... Woowoowoo? Woooo, wooowoo wooowooowooo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well if you are going to take that attitude, then I have nothing further to say on the matter, and bid you good day!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I don't want to brag, but seriously: I am SOOOO rich....

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Woo! WOO WOOOOO WOOWOOWOO WOO WOOOOO!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I know they say money doesn't bring you happiness, but sometimes I just like to open my bank account and look at all of the stuff I own and just think to myself:

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow. I am soooooo rich.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Wooowoowoooooo.... Woowoowoo? Woooo, wooowoo wooowooowooo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, us rich alive people don't want to waste all day spending time with you poor dead people, because I'm just sooooo rich I have to go now, and very possibly make myself even richer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See ya around ghosty!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't say?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Woo! WOO WOOOOO WOOWOOWOO WOO WOOOOO!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't say!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Wooowoowoooooo.... Woowoowoo? Woooo, wooowoo wooowooowooo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I guess you didn't say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah it's not bad, but I prefer cooked chicken.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Woo! WOO WOOOOO WOOWOOWOO WOO WOOOOO!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe, but nothing beats a home cooked pie!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Man, I love pie!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Wooowoowoooooo.... Woowoowoo? Woooo, wooowoo wooowooowooo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't say? I never knew that. Well, I must be going, see you around.

sections.title: Talking to any Mysterious ghost in the wrong location

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're a ghost, huh?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Apparently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In which case I only have one thing to say to you:

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Woooo! Woowooooowoo, wooo WOOOOOOOOOOO woowoo woowoowoo woo!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: ...what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Guess you don't have the amulet of humanspeak, huh?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: ...huh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, that's right. WOO! WOOOWOOO WOO WOOOOWOO! Got no comeback for that, have you?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: You are very strange...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Mortal... Take heed of my example, and waste not your life, lest you may suffer the same fate as myself...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh? You mean someone did this to you?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: You have ascertained the truth in my words...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So who did it to you? And why?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Events of moons past, I remember not clearly...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine help you are then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Please... Leave me to my fate. I have been here so long that I scarce remember aught else...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh.... huh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: You can see me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... yes?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: And you understand my words?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, most of them...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: This is incredible! How can such a thing have come to pass?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I say? I'm a professional.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Hello back at you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's a nice ghost like you doing in a place like this?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I suppose you think that's funny?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... Mildly amusing I guess.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I don't think I want to talk to you anymore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Hello stranger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So.... Invisible ghost haunting the same place for thousands of years, huh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's got to be a pretty rubbish way to spend your life. Er, death.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: You have no idea...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, bad luck and all that. See ya around!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Hello stranger. It is rare indeed that I meet one who can see my presence, let alone one who can understand my words.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Soooo.... Is it fun being a ghost?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Does it look like fun to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... Well, yes actually.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Then you are a fool, and I will waste no more words upon you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, not even 'goodbye'?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sheesh, what a grouch. You'd think you'd have more of a sense of humour being dead and all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: The endless tragedy of fate... Why must you torment me so?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, alright, calm down, calm down. All I said was 'hello'!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Thank you for hearing my tale... It has been so lonely here...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you remind me where to find the thief Rennard who caused this curse to befall you again?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Of course...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, the infamous Rennard... The last I had heard of that vile thief, he had joined a group of bandits in an evil land to the North-east of here, where they had made their home living outside of

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: the reach of the authorities that pursued them...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, the infamous Rennard... The last I had heard of him, he had sought passage on a ship crewed by none but the most dastardly lowly pirates...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I also heard that this ship had been caught in a violent storm, and stranded upon rocks, where the pirates then made their home...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, the infamous Rennard... The last I had heard of that vile thief, he had joined a group of bandits in a barren land to the South-east of here, where they prey upon the unsuspecting visitors to

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: the desert awaiting the return of their dark master...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: H-hello!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's up?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I cannot believe it! You can see me? You understand my words?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure can. So why are you hanging around here?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: My tale is one of woe... No doubt you will have little interest in hearing it...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Though it has been so many moons since last I had company in this endless non-life...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, actually I would like to know what happened to you to turn you into an invisible ghost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If only so I can make sure it doesn't happen to me...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: My name is Valdez. I served my Lord Saradomin faithfully for many years, as an explorer of this strange land we had been brought to.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I remember the day this curse fell upon me clearly... I had just discovered a huge temple, hidden below the ground, of one of Saradomin's compatriots.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I am unsure who had built it, or why they had left it seemingly abandoned, but inside I located a great treasure...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: It was the godstaff of Armadyl. Oh, how I rue my choice that day!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Choice?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Aye, stranger. I chose that day to take it so that my Lord Saradomin's power and prestige could be increased by its possession.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: A god-weapon! Do you have any comprehension of the difficulty and rarity in obtaining such a thing?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: To find such an artefact of power just lying around, it is almost incomprehensible...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: So it was there in that deserted temple that I made my choice. I took the staff, and left that temple for Entrana immediately.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: This was the cause of my cursed state.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, you mean you gave it to Saradomin and in return he cursed you???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seems kind of ungrateful if you ask me...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: No stranger, you misunderstand completely... Firstly my gracious Lord would never treat anyone in such a manner; If he felt it was beyond my bounds as a mere mortal

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: to hold such an artefact, he would simply have commanded me to return it to whence I had claimed it, and I being eternally loyal would have obeyed without question...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And secondly?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: And secondly, I never managed to pass the artefact on to my Lord...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: The vile thief Rennard accosted me as I made my way to Entrana, and after defeating me with a sneak attack, plundered the staff from my person, and left me for dead...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I do not know what became of the staff, but I can feel in my very bones that whatever its final fate was, it is somehow related to this curse upon me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow. Tough break.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I am sorry to bore you with my tale stranger, please allow me to compound my rudeness by asking you for one favour, small to perform?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eh, I won't make any promises, but if it's nothing too annoying I guess I can help you out.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Many thanks stranger, this existence tortures me...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I need you to find Rennard and if he has the staff yet reclaim it, or find out what hideous deed he performed to curse me so!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I have nothing I may offer you save this piece of clothing, please take it as payment...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find this Rennard then?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, the infamous Rennard... The last I had heard of that vile thief, he had joined a group of bandits in an evil land to the North-east of here, where they had made their home living outside of

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: the reach of the authorities that pursued them...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, the infamous Rennard... The last I had heard of him, he had sought passage on a ship crewed by none but the most dastardly lowly pirates...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I also heard that this ship had been caught in a violent storm, and stranded upon rocks, where the pirates then made their home...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, the infamous Rennard... The last I had heard of that vile thief, he had joined a group of bandits in a barren land to the South-east of here, where they prey upon the unsuspecting visitors to

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: the desert awaiting the return of their dark master...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's all fascinating, but I just don't particularly care. Bye-bye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Rennard.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, it be you again! What can I do fer ya?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me where I can find Kharrim again?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Kharrim the messenger... Well, he was always a devoted follower of old General Zamorak, and if I remember rightly Zamorak set up a small base in an old temple near Dareeyak...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: You might want to check around there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Kharrim the messenger... Last I'd heard of him, he'd headed off to Carrallagar to seek his fortune. Ya might want to check around there somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Kharrim the messenger... The last I'd heard of that weasel he was claiming he'd found some underground deposit of runite ore guarded by demons and dragons.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I suspect he was pulling some scam or other, but if you know of such a place, that might be a good place to start checking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. You must be Rennard.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: What be this? You both see me and hear me, and also know my name?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Tell me what devilry brings you here, and be quick about it afore I gut you like a fish!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, apart from the fact I ain't scared of no ghost, I am here because I have spoken to Valdez.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Valdez? Who be that? Some foul necromancer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, he was a ghost I met near Glarial's tomb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He seems convinced that the artefact you stole from him is responsible for him becoming cursed to be an invisible ghost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seems like he might be onto something too, given the state of you.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: A curse ye say... Aye, that makes sense...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: And there was I thinking my fate be the fault of the thieving and murdering I spent me life a-doing...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: So it all began the day I stole that staff, ye say? Aye, that be a story I have never told another soul...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why don't you tell me what happened? I might be able to help...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well, I was making me merry way along, having just pulled off a glorious jewellery heist from a bunch of stinking dwarves...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, that's no way to talk about dwarves! Some of my best friends are short!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, yer misunderstand me [lad/lass], I wasn't generalising about the whole dwarf species, I had just stolen a bundle of jewels from a very specific group of dwarves who happened to have an odious stench about them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Well I guess that's okay then. Please continue.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well, as I headed on me merry way, hoping the foul odours that lingered in me nostrils would soon pass, I see in front of me this explorer fella, all decked out in in his fine clothing, and carrying some long package

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: bundled in rags.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: So I says to meself, 'Rennard', I says, 'Rennard, why would some fella all dressed in his finery be carrying something wrapped in dirty rags?'.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: So I thinks to meself a little more, 'Rennard', I thinks, 'Rennard, maybe that fella has something valuable in there, and he covered it in dirty rags so it don't look so valuable'.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: So I coshed this fella round the back of his head with me bag of jewels, picked up his package and was on me merry way afore he comes to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happened then?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well, I makes me way to the closest tavern I knew of that catered to my sort of people...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean thieves?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Right ye are, so I makes me way to the nearest friendly tavern, and unwraps the bundle to see what it had inside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Staff of Armadyl?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Was it? Ah, I never knew that...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Anysways, I unwraps this staff, and sees it be a god- weapon; I may be just a common thief, but I recognises a weapon not made by mortal hands when I sees one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what did you do then?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well, I knew such a weapon would be of great value to...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Now that's funny. Can't remember his name, now. The powerful god, lived in the North-east. Took the Mahjarrat away from under Icthlarin's

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: control.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Anyway, I hired me a messenger to go off and let him know I had something I was prepared to sell that I thought he'd be interested in...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Now WHY can't I remember his name? Very odd that...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you sold the staff to this god you can't remember?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well, that's the other funny thing... He never showed up, he sent some General or other instead.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Hmmm... You know... Thinking back on that, I'm getting the feeling that messenger did a little doublecross of his own, and took

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: me message to the wrong fella.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what was this General's name?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: His name was Zamorak. I remember thinking at the time it was odd, because the fella was a mighty powerful warrior, but was never fully trusted by...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: WHY can't I remember his name???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you suspect the messenger might have taken the message to the wrong person? So you think it was an accident or deliberate?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well that I can't tell ya, but if something happened to get me cursed, it's likely the messenger would know what more than me.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: His name was Kharrim, and if he caused me to be stuck like this, I'm gonna fillet him like a dog, ghost or no!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I tell ye what, you've given me much to think about so I'd like to offer yer a gift; Here, take these, they were the gloves I stole me first cake with, they might bring yer some luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find this Kharrim then?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Kharrim the messenger... Well, he was always a devoted follower of old General Zamorak, and if I remember rightly Zamorak set up a small base in an old temple near Dareeyak...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: You might want to check around there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Kharrim the messenger... Last I'd heard of him, he'd headed off to Carrallagar to seek his fortune. Ya might want to check around there somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Kharrim the messenger... The last I'd heard of that weasel he was claiming he'd found some underground deposit of runite ore guarded by demons and dragons.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I suspect he was pulling some scam or other, but if you know of such a place, that might be a good place to start checking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's all fascinating, but I just don't particularly care. Bye-bye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Kharrim.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you remind me where to find Lennissa?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah yes, the whereabouts of the treacherous Lennissa...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well, we knew little about her or she would have been caught and exposed as a traitor and a spy, but Lucien did mention that he had evidence that she was a great fan of ball games...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well, she was always sickeningly obedient to Saradomin, so I would expect her to have run to some great place of worship of him if she was affected by the curse to try and gain his blessing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: According to Lucien's intelligence report, she had been uncovered as a spy by her constant use of ships to ferry information...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: It is entirely possible she would be located somewhere coastal to this day!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. So you must be Kharrim the messenger.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: How do you know my name, stranger?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well now... I had a very interesting chat with Rennard the thief.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems you redirected his message regarding a certain god-weapon for your own ends.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: So THAT is what this is about... I should have known the deal was too good to have no repercussions...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems as though you might be responsible for this curse that has befallen you by not delivering Rennard's message to the correct person.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ha! That is not a truthful assessment of the story... You might think differently if you had heard my side of events.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please let me hear your side of the story then...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well, if you have spoken to Rennard, then you will know that he had somehow managed to obtain a very valuable weapon, and was looking for buyers.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: What he probably didn't tell you, was that he met me in a drunken stupor in some smoke filled tavern, and I offered to arrange a purchaser for his item, in exchange for a small finders fee.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you knew what the staff was?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: The god-staff of Armadyl? Well, of course I did.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Honestly, you would have to be pretty slow-witted to not recognise a legendary artefact such as that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, I don't understand, Rennard said that he had a buyer already in mind, and that you diverted his message to a General Zamorak instead?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ha! Here is a word of advice for you adventurer; Never trust the words of a drunk.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Whatever he might have thought he was doing with it, in the end all that happened was he left me to arrange a purchaser for the item.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you thought you would offer it to General Zamorak?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah yes, Lord Zamorak. He was merely a mortal back then, you know?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Yet I could see great things in store for him even then. He had a kind of brilliant ruthlessness... And that special kind of vicious streak you see so rarely...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well anyway, when given the task of selling a weapon forged by the very gods themselves, I naturally thought of Zamorak as a potential buyer.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I was a messenger in his employ anyway, so it was a mere trifle to find him and deliver the news, and I knew of his particular interest in armour and weaponry of all kinds.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Yes, he was always quite the connoisseur when it came to weaponry...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: But I digress. I let Lord Zamorak know that there was some drunken fool with an artefact of incredible power that could probably be bought off with a few jewels and trinkets,

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: and he escorted me to the tavern and made the purchase there and then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: It was a satisfactory deal all around, I got a share of the sale price from Rennard, and I greatly increased my prestige amongst Zamorak and his followers.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: But maybe... Perhaps the events that followed were responsible for my cursed state...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Events that followed?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I can not tell you of them precisely, for I myself was not there to witness them.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I am after all, simply a messenger. When... 'it' happened, I was busy elsewhere delivering a message from Zamorak to the rest of his Mahjarrat ilk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When 'it' happened? What was 'it'?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: As I have explained, I was not there, and I do not know what Zamorak did with the staff, but whatever it was resulted in his banishment by the other gods for many years.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: The very strange thing was that Saradomin must have known about it, whatever it was, before it even happened...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? Why do you say that?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well, it was the contents of the message I was returning to Zamorak;

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Lucien seemed quite certain that there was a spy for Saradomin somewhere amongst his followers named Lennissa.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: If whatever happened to the staff caused this curse to befall me, then it is certain that she too would have been afflicted, because she would have been in the very heart of the action.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Hmmm....

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: You have given me much to think on adventurer. I would like to reward you with my sturdy messenger boots, may they aid you in your travels.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But where can I find this Lennissa?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah yes, the whereabouts of the treacherous Lennissa...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well, we knew little about her or she would have been caught and exposed as a traitor and a spy, but Lucien did mention that he had evidence that she was a great fan of ball games...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well, she was always sickeningly obedient to Saradomin, so I would expect her to have run to some great place of worship of him if she was affected by the curse to try and gain his blessing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: According to Lucien's intelligence report, she had been uncovered as a spy by her constant use of ships to ferry information...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: It is entirely possible she would be located somewhere coastal to this day!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's all fascinating, but I just don't particularly care. Bye-bye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Lennissa. Can you remind me where to find Dhalak?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Dhalak? I know not where, but he would try and make the most of his situation if he has been cursed, and find a place to lift his spirits!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Dhalak? Well, he was always a knowlegeable mage, so if this curse has befallen him as well, I would suspect he would be researching how to free himself of it.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I would look for a library to find him if I were you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Dhalak? He was always a loyal follower of Saradomin... I think he would have found an altar to Saradomin so that he may pray for this curse to be lifted.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. You would be Lennissa, I take it?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Who are you? Where did you hear that name? How comes it that you can see and speak to me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, a ghost called Kharrim directed me towards you.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: So that weasel Kharrim has been blighted by this curse too?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ha, a good thing too. If anybody deserved such a fate it would be one such as him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess you didn't get along then?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: No, evil scum such as he should never have been allowed to walk this world.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: What lies has he told you to come here? Have you come to try and kill me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm not sure I could if I tried, but that is not why I have come to you.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Then speak, and speak well, for I may yet be dead, but am still a danger to those who cross me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'm trying to work out why all of you invisible ghosts seem to have been cursed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure how exactly, but the trail seems to have led me to you...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: That makes no sense... I served Saradomin faithfully my entire life, then all of a sudden I find myself reduced to this state!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it seems as though that may have been the cause...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: What? Explain yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As I understand it, it was something to do with the Staff of Armadyl, and your refusal to tell Saradomin that it had been stolen...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: What? But... But that is not how it happened at all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then please, go ahead and tell me the events of that day in your own words...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Let me see... I had been working as a spy for my Lord Saradomin, amongst the forces of... the Empty Lord.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'The Empty Lord'? Who is that?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I will not give you his name, for to do so would give him power here.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Let us just say that he was a fearsome deity, whose strength was greater than all the gods we knew of on this realm at the time.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: It is probably worth mentioning that at this time we had no knowledge of the mysterious nature god Guthix.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So he was stronger than Saradomin?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: As Saradomin was, yes. As Saradomin is now? Who can say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see... Please, continue.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: As I say, I was working as a spy within the very camp of my Lord's enemies.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I knew that should I have been caught, I risked being killed upon the spot, but my combat skills were always formidable, and if truth be told, there was a fair amount of dissent amongst... 'his' followers anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you mean 'dissent'?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, to not understand this, you must have led a sheltered life...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Let me tell you this: Evil will always breed more evil, and will never be satisfied with what it has.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: The Empty Lord chose to ally himself with the dark creatures of this world, fully aware that their own natures would cause them to rally against his rule, and take every opportunity they could to betray him.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: This has always been the nature of evil. Perhaps he thought his power could prevent such treachery?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: This allowed me freedom amongst their camp, for it was always easy to point the finger of suspicion at some unsuspecting necromancer or foolish Mahjarrat if it seemed as though my activities had been discovered.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Similarly, should I ever be caught in the act of my sabotage, it was all too easy to bribe whoever found me or persuade them into believing it was just some minor treachery of my own, rather than my work for my

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Lord Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... Well, that makes sense, but I don't understand what the Staff of Armadyl had to do with this...?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: As I have told you, the Empty Lord was extremely powerful, but not so powerful that he could rule over the other deities of this world without opposition.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Should he have made a move against any other god, then he could still have been easily brought down by the combined efforts of the others.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: The theft of Armadyl's staff changed this however.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: If he had taken possession of this god-weapon, then his power would have been so great that he could have overthrown all on this world, and made it into his own image!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I could not allow such a thing to happen!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I went immediately to my comrade Dhalak, the mage, and told him that a message had come to the lair offering this weapon for sale!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I knew that as soon as my Lord Saradomin heard this, he would contact Armadyl to inform them of the theft, and the matter would have been resolved quickly and discreetly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you passed this information to Dhalak instead of taking it to Saradomin yourself?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: To my eternal shame, I indeed failed my Lord Saradomin...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I could not risk taking the message directly, for I feared my disguise had been uncovered.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Lucien particularly had been taking an unhealthy interest in my activities, and I had a gut feeling that to make any obvious moves against the Empty Lord would have been my undoing.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: But Dhalak was a noble man! I cannot believe that he would not have taken my message immediately to Lord Saradomin!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it seems like he didn't, but I don't know why not...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Please adventurer, discover what foul fate must have befallen him for him to have neglected his duty!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I have not much to offer as reward, but for these spare robes I wore while on assignment...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Please find him and discover why I am cursed like this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would I be able to find this Dhalak then?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Dhalak? I know not where, but he would try and make the most of his situation if he has been cursed, and find a place to lift his spirits!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Dhalak? Well, he was always a knowlegeable mage, so if this curse has befallen him as well, I would suspect he would be researching how to free himself of it.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I would look for a library to find him if I were you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Dhalak? He was always a loyal follower of Saradomin... I think he would have found an altar to Saradomin so that he may pray for this curse to be lifted.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's all fascinating, but I just don't particularly care. Bye-bye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dhalak, where can I find the swordsman Viggora?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, the evil swordsman Viggora... A rogue like him would probably flock to his own kind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, the evil swordsman Viggora... Perhaps he has returned to his castle in the dark lands?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, the evil swordsman Viggora... Paddewwa was where he fought many battles, perhaps he has returned to one of his old haunts?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Dhalak.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: You see my form, hear my words, and know my name, yet your face I recognise not...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Be you some mighty sorcerer to bind me so?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... Well, not really...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I don't mean to brag, but I guess I am with my level 99 magic...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But that is besides the point. It is not I who has trapped you here as a ghost.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Then how comes it to be that you know my name stranger?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lennissa told me about you, and where to find you?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Lennissa? Oh that poor sweet girl... Has my foolishness cursed her as well as myself???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your foolishness?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: The story shames me stranger, I wouldst rather keep it unto myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I don't want to force you into telling me, but perhaps sharing it with someone might relieve your guilt?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Aye... Perhaps it might at that.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: So what has Lennissa told you of the events of the day this curse befell me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, she told me that she was working as an undercover agent of Saradomin amongst the followers of some 'Empty Lord', and when news of the theft of a god-weapon reached her, she passed the message on to

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: you instead of taking it to Saradomin because she was scared her cover might be blown.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Aye, that is a fair account of events...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't understand why you didn't take her message to Saradomin?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Stranger, my foolishness was a result of my respect for Saradomin, not as a result of any attempted treachery!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why didn't you pass on Lennissa's message? As I understand it something happened with that staff that could have been avoided if you had passed her message on!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: (sigh) I know not what occurred with that god-weapon, but I have my suspicions...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Let me explain myself. I was Lennissa's immediate superior, and I was often her contact for missions.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Because of this role, I had access to a larger picture of what was happening than she herself did, and I was not only well aware that her presence amongst the enemy camp had been detected, but I was also well aware that

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: there was a growing faction amongst them who were plotting to overthrow their master.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Their master being...?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: That I will not tell you. I will tempt the fates no more than I already have done.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: But anyway, it had come to my attention that the Mahjarrat who had been liberated from the control of Icthlarin did not much appreciate one form of slavery to another, and under the leadership of the mighty

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Zamorak were making plans to overthrow their master and take his power for themselves.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Now, as powerful, long-lived and evil as they were, they were still just mortal, and I made the decision that it would be of benefit to my Lord Saradomin for his mightiest rival to be distracted by such internal conflicts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So that is why you decided not to pass the report from Lennissa on?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Yes, but my guilt is more than simply inaction...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I knew that with such a weapon, Zamorak would be capable of launching an attack that could actually stand a chance of success, but I also knew that he would never be able to get a chance to use it in a battle for

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: being a god-weapon its very presence would have sung out to their leader.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm guessing you did something about that, then?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Indeed I did. To my eternal shame, I decided that I would assist Zamorak and his henchmen in their battle, by secretly casting a spell of concealment upon the staff so that

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: they might use it secretly against their master.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So Zamorak knew about this?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: No, nobody except myself, and now you, knew that I cast such a spell....

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Had I known what a threat to my Lord Saradomin Zamorak would later become, I wouldst have taken the message to Saradomin immediately! Alas, it is all too easy to see your mistakes after you

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: have made them...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm confused. What exactly happened with this staff anyway? And why have all these various random people been cursed because of it?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I cannot answer your question with anything other than my own suppositions, but I do know of one who might be able to, and if any man deserved to be cursed for their actions that day, it was he!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you speaking of?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: His name was Viggora. He was an evil man, brutal and vicious, and deadly with a blade.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: He was one of the few humans Zamorak allowed to rise to a position of power amongst his rebels, possibly because he imitated those same qualities of Zamorak.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: If anyone knows what Zamorak did with that god- weapon to have caused this curse to have befallen us, it would have been he, for he would have been fighting on Zamorak's very right hand side in their rebellion.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Please, if this curse can be lifted, find Viggora and find out what he has wrought upon us! I have no wealth nor magic to aid you, but take my hood as reward;

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: it has served me well these centuries past, and may bring you luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would you suggest I look for Viggora?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, the evil swordsman Viggora... A rogue like him would probably flock to his own kind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, the evil swordsman Viggora... Perhaps he has returned to his castle in the dark lands?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, the evil swordsman Viggora... Paddewwa was where he fought many battles, perhaps he has returned to one of his old haunts?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well I'll try and find him for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's all fascinating, but I just don't particularly care. Bye-bye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Hello yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I really liked your little story. Can you tell me another one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Preferably something with a big fight and lots of explosions!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: ...You are a very strange young [man/woman].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I have more of those ghostly robes you gave me?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: It seems as though the curse that keeps me here extends to my very clothing... which piece do you want?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... You must be the infamous Viggora.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Hold thy tongue varlet! Speak fast, how come you to find me here, and how doth you understand mine speech?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want me to hold my tongue and tell you how I found you?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Cease thy chatter and respond to my demand!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cease my chatter AND respond to your demand?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I warn thee knave, this curse upon me hath not improved my temper, these centuries past...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it's actually about that curse that I have come to speak to you.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Oh, be that so? Then forgive my swift anger, and speak to me of how you plan to break this curse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... I didn't actually mention anything about breaking the curse...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Then what lets you dare speak to me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I heard of your name from a mage called Dhalak, and I'm trying to find out what exactly caused the curse to befall him, and you as well apparently.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ha! So the weak-willed mage was cursed along with me?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well now, that is an interesting turn of events... Then am I to assume that Valdez, Rennard, Kharrim and Lennissa were also cursed along with me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...How did you know that?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ha ha ha! Oh, the curse cut deeper than I had previously thought!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Stranger, this news has brought me a ray of sunshine in an otherwise dreary millennium! Please, ask any question you wish!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... Thanks, I think. So what exactly happened on that day you were all cursed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know that Valdez discovered the Staff of Armadyl, was robbed by Rennard, who then sent Kharrim to tell Zamorak of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Meanwhile Lennissa heard of the sale, and informed Dhalak who placed an enchantment upon it so that its power would be hidden.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still don't know what happened with the staff to cause this curse, or what you had to do with it though...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well stranger, rest yourself awhile, and I will recount a tale of the events of that day, for I was one of the few actually there when it happened...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When what happened?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: When my Lord Zamorak first got his taste of godhood!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow. Sounds like quite the dinner party anecdote.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: You can take the snide venom out of your voice whelp, you came to me; 'twas not the other way round.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay. Please continue.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well now, let us see... As you may have heard tell, my affiliation lay with General Zamorak, a mighty warrior of the Mahjarrat tribe, and my skill on the battlefield had quickly brought

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: me to his attention.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: So pleased was he with my bloodlust that he promoted me on the battlefield once to serve in his honour guard, and let me tell you, this was a rare honour indeed, for I was the only human chosen to take such a position.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Oh yes, the dragon riders, Mahjarrat, demons and vampyre warriors made up the bulk of the force, but I wager I was their equal in all ways of combat.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ha, when I think of someone like Lucien struggling to lift a blade, in some ways I was even their better!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please continue.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well anyway... Myself and the rest of Zamoraks honour guard were formulating stratagems in our battle-tent, when that sneaky messenger Kharrim came in offering to sell us

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: the god-staff of Armadyl!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Naturally, we suspected that this was some trick by our Lord to test our loyalty...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, who was your lord? Everyone has been very evasive about that...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Quiet fool, all things in their course; You are disrupting my train of thought!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well anyway, we thought it was too good to be true, yet when we visited this scummy tavern we were amazed to discover there was no trick, no test of loyalty, no hidden trap:

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Somehow this fool had actually managed to obtain the god-staff of Armadyl!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Its power was incredible, you could almost feel the energies crackling around it in the air!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happened then?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well, with such a weapon, the plans we had been developing for a rebellion against our lord could finally be put into action, but we knew that we would have to act swiftly, before he heard that we had a weapon

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: capable of defeating him, and we would have to act decisively, for even amongst our group there were still those loyal to the lord - such as that pathetic fool Azzanadra.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So JUST WHO WAS this lord you speak of?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: And I tell thee again, I will say when it is appropriate, now do not disrupt my tale!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, carry on then...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: So anyway, Lord Zamorak and his most trusted compatriots, namely myself, Hazeel, Drakan, Thammaron and Zemouregal made plans to overthrow our lord using the god-weapon, and by pledging

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: allegiance to Zamorak as our master, were each to be given a large piece of land as our own in return.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: We decided to move immediately, before anyone got cold feet, or any other parties could interfere in our work, and made haste towards the castle where our Lord lived.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: If Lucien had not been otherwise occupied, he would have probably accompanied us with his magicks, but it turned out the foolish Dhalak had made his involvement unnecessary with some spells of his own allowing us to

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: get close enough to the castle with the staff without the Empty Lord being able to sense its presence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So your lord was the one that cursed you?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I am coming to that... So anyway, we made our way to the castle, under the pretense that we had war plans against Saradomin and the other deities to discuss.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: As usual, our lord was guarded well, but this was why Zamorak had brought his most trusted fighters with him.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: While we distracted the Empty Lord with our feints and attacks, and kept his bodyguards busy, Lord Zamorak outflanked him, unsheathed the staff and plunged it into his back!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Ah, it was a glorious sight... At that moment I was reminded for whom I fought, and why General Zamorak had earned his nickname 'the scourge' upon the battlefield...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And the next thing you know you were cursed?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: No, it was not quite that simple... The Empty Lord turned away from our battle, eyes burning with hatred, and towards Zamorak instead. Seeing this, we all fought with extra vigour, so that

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: General Zamorak would not face our lord alone, but we were outnumbered by many hundreds of warriors and demons, and could not reach him to assist him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what then?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Why, it was the Empty Lord versus Zamorak, in single combat! And the sight of the battle will be with me forever more...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: The Empty Lord was a powerful god, stronger than any of the others awake at that time, possibly even as strong as Guthix is, and Zamorak was but a mortal: A Mahjarrat warrior all the same, with all of the

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: strength and power that that entails, but mortal nonetheless, but to see him fight, you would not think of him as a 'mere' anything...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: He was war itself! Flurry after flurry of blows he rained upon the Empty Lord, and the very castle walls shook and quivered with their power, but the Empty Lord would not fall!

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Even with the weapon of a god embedded in his back, he fought on, and with each blow our victory seemed less and less certain...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what then?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well, then a miracle happened. Or luck. Or natural justice.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: You can call it what you want, but as the Empty Lords hands wrapped tightly around Zamorak's throat, Lord Zamorak, kicking and screaming defiantly and radiant in his anger until the very last, plunged towards the

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Empty Lord, who seemed to lose his footing slightly, and fell in such a way so that the staff plunged deeper into his body, but also impaled Lord Zamorak with it at the same time...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: And then...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then what?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: And then nothing. There was a sudden flash of bright light, and then a sudden blink of cold darkness, and it was over.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Zamorak stood over the Empty Lord who was slowly... fading from existence... And as he faded, it seemed as though... It almost seemed as though Zamorak became more real,

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: more solid than he had been before...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: And as the Empty Lord faded from this world completely, I heard his voice, almost a whisper upon the wind, cursing all who had helped Zamorak in his victory, which as you now tell me seems to have been all who

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: were responsible for the staff ending up in Zamorak's hands at the castle.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: As I heard it, I saw that I too was fading, just as the Empty Lord had, and I called to my brethren for their assistance, but they could no longer hear my words, nor see my form.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: It was then that the other gods appeared and banished Zamorak from the world completely for daring to kill one of their kind, although as it turned out it didn't quite work out that way for them, when he returned

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: stronger than ever, a god himself.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: But the god wars were another story entirely...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... I don't understand... If it was Zamorak who used the weapon, then why was it only you who were cursed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And the other people who were cursed, why them? Why not the other Mahjarrat for example?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And just who was this 'Empty Lord' you keep speaking of?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Well, in my life I was nothing but a warrior. I had no hidden knowledge, I didn't especially care about the gods or their magics, and I certainly didn't respect them.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Now in my... I suppose this is my death. I am but a shade on the wind, unnoticed by all who pass me, until today anyway, and the only answer I can give you is that the others who were with me, the

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Mahjarrats and the Vampyre, they were beings of magic.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: It runs through their very veins, and ebbs through their bones.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Who knows why the curse fell as it did? Perhaps as a mere human I was more susceptible to it, when they were not. Perhaps they too are cursed, but their life spans are so

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: long that it will be millennia before they feel it upon them. Perhaps because it did not affect them, it extended backwards through time, to the moment the staff was

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: taken from its rightful place, and all who had known of its theft were cursed too. Perhaps there are others also cursed, who played no part in this tale, and were merely unlucky enough to be

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: in the wrong place at the wrong time...

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I don't know. Things do not always happen for a reason, just as tales do not always end with all of the loose ends neatly tied up and all answers supplied.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: I have simply told you the events I was witness to, for I can do no more.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: You have cheered me no end to let me know that this curse that has afflicted me has not left me alone here, in this void between worlds.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Perhaps I will hunt down these others who have also been cursed as I was, but I feel I must reward you for your efforts; Here, take my cloak, it is drenched in the blood of a

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: thousand foes, and may bring you luck in battle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how can I break this curse?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: Who knows? If it was the death curse of the Empty Lord, there may be no way to break it.

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: If it was not his death curse, and he is still alive but not on this world, then the only way to break it may be to bring him back here;

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: But I would rather stay cursed than suffer under his rule again...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But WHO was this 'Empty Lord'? WHAT was his NAME?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: You do not know? You have not guessed yet?

lines.speaker: Mysterious ghost
lines.text: He was Zaros.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's all fascinating, but I just don't particularly care. Bye-bye.
